summary: Partially decompiled and matched `ftColl_80078C70` in fighter collision code, replacing a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` WIP C stub with a large structured C translation while keeping the original assembly under `MUST_MATCH`. The PR also renamed the local hit capsule accessor from `ftHitGetPtr` to `HitCapsuleGetPtr` and updated `HitCapsule` layout in `lbcollision.h` with a union exposing the same storage as either `owner` or the bitfield `hit_grabbed_victim_only`. The body reports this resolved part of issue #595 and improved `ftColl_80078C70` to 80.46% match, but the PR contains no review comments.
what_changed: Changed the helper `ftHitGetPtr(Fighter* fp, u32 i)` to `HitCapsuleGetPtr(Fighter* fp, u32 i)` and updated call sites in `ftColl_80078A2C` and the new WIP body for `ftColl_80078C70`.
Replaced the non-implemented WIP body of `ftColl_80078C70(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` with a large partial C implementation using `this_gobj`, `this_fp`, `victim_gobj`, and `victim_fp` terminology.

Implemented traversal of `HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters` to compare the current fighter against possible victim fighters, including self-skip/self-order tracking through `is_same_gobj` and `next_gobj` control flow.
Added logic for player/team filtering using `gm_8016B1C4`, `gm_8016B168`, `gm_8016B0D4`, `player_id`, `x61B_team`, `x119C_teamUnk`, `x2225_b4`, and thrown-hitbox ownership fields.

Added hitbox eligibility checks over `Fighter.x914` hit capsules, including disabled/catch/inert element checks, ground/air masks, `x40_b0`, `x40_b2`, `x40_b3`, `x42_b5`, `x43_b2`, and `lbColl_8000ACFC` gating.

Added collision paths for hitbox-vs-hitbox, shield collision, and hurtbox collision using calls such as `lbColl_80007AFC`, `ftColl_8007699C`, `lbColl_80007BCC`, `lbColl_8000805C`, `ftColl_80076CBC`, and `ftColl_80076ED8`.
Added handling for inert hit elements by setting fields such as `victim_fp->x221C_b5` and `victim_fp->unk_gobj` rather than following normal hit reaction paths.
Added hit sound/effect related logic through `ft_80088148`, local static `hit_sfx[20]`, `lbColl_80005BB0`, `x215C`, `x2160`, `sfx_kind`, and `sfx_severity`.
Modified `struct HitCapsule` in `lbcollision.h` so the previous `HSD_GObj* owner` member is represented in a union with a `u8 hit_grabbed_victim_only : 1` bitfield overlay.
smart_moves: Kept the decompiled body under `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` while retaining the original assembly in the `#else` branch, allowing partial progress without claiming a full non-WIP match.
Used `sizeof(this_fp->x914) / sizeof(HitCapsule)` instead of hard-coding the hit capsule count in new loops, making the code more resilient to array-size knowledge encoded in the struct.
Introduced `HitCapsuleGetPtr` as a more type/descriptive accessor name than `ftHitGetPtr`, matching the data type being returned rather than tying the helper to a specific subsystem prefix.

Modeled an observed overlapping field in `HitCapsule` with a union between `owner` and `hit_grabbed_victim_only`, preserving existing access while enabling the decompiled collision logic to refer to the bit interpretation needed by `ftColl_80078C70`.

Used explicit `goto` labels such as `next_gobj`, `block_16`, `block_66`, and `block_81` to preserve difficult compiler control flow in a partial match instead of prematurely rewriting into cleaner but less matchable structured C.
Preserved questionable or unresolved regions with comments, including the phantom-hit/hurtbox-priority comment block, making uncertainty visible for later cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter-collision routines, partial matching may require accepting messy control flow, temporaries, casts, and labels before attempting semantic cleanup.
When an assembly field appears to be used both as a pointer and as a flag/bitfield, a union in the shared header can expose both interpretations without changing offsets.
Renaming helpers to describe the returned data structure, such as `HitCapsuleGetPtr`, can make newly decompiled code easier to search and reuse across nearby collision routines.

Ground/air gating and disabled/catch/inert hit-element checks recur heavily in fighter collision code; matching future functions should reuse the same condition patterns and enum names where evidence supports them.
The function body shows that hitbox, shield, and hurtbox collision paths are intertwined; future decomp work should avoid isolating these too early if doing so changes compiler branch shape.
For WIP decompilation, keeping static tables such as `hit_sfx[20]` declared locally can help express known data references even when the exact initializer or symbol provenance is not yet resolved.

naming_conventions.lesson: Use explicit current-fighter versus victim-fighter naming in collision routines to keep nested fighter traversal understandable.
Accessor names can be centered on the struct type being accessed rather than the older subsystem-specific helper name `ftHitGetPtr`.
Bitfield names should encode the inferred gameplay meaning when the header needs to expose a flag overlaying an existing storage slot.
Compiler-block labels are acceptable in WIP matching code when they preserve branch layout and communicate that the structure is not yet cleaned up.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Retained the original `ASM` implementation for `ftColl_80078C70` behind the `MUST_MATCH` path while adding a C body for WIP/non-MUST_MATCH builds.
Used many explicit casts to `u8`, `u32`, and `s32` around bitfields, enum comparisons, and boolean tests to influence generated code shape.

Preserved temporary-style variables such as `var_r0_2`, `var_r22`, `var_r17`, `var_r4`, `var_r3`, `temp_r23`, and `temp_r16`, suggesting register/assembly-guided translation rather than fully cleaned source.
Used `goto` labels to reproduce branch targets and avoid over-structuring a complex collision routine.
Added a header union to make a bitfield access compile against the same offset as an existing pointer field, a struct-layout tactic for matching code that treats the same memory in different ways.
The PR report says `ftColl_80078C70` reached 80.46% with score `7,406` of max `37,900`, indicating a partial rather than complete match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match or further clean up `ftColl_80078C70`.
Search for subsequent changes to `HitCapsule` union fields `owner` and `hit_grabbed_victim_only` to confirm whether the overlay survived later review.
Look for later renames or uses of `HitCapsuleGetPtr` across fighter collision files.
Investigate issue #595 to understand the broader set of fighter collision functions this PR was resolving.
Compare later versions of the phantom-hit comment in `ftColl_80078C70` to see whether that control-flow uncertainty was resolved.
